---
title: "Table 2: Graduation Rate Distribution"
output: html_document
---

```{r, echo=FALSE, results='asis'}
library(kableExtra)

data <- data.frame(
  Demographic_Group = c("Asian/Pacific Share", "Black Share", "Hispanic Share", "Non-resident Alien Share"),
  Min = c(0.00000, 0.00000, 0.00000, 0.00000),
  `1st_Qu.` = c(0.34485, 0.34485, 0.34485, 0.34485),
  Median = c(0.50000, 0.50000, 0.50000, 0.50000),
  Mean = c(0.50085, 0.50085, 0.50085, 0.50085),
  `3rd_Qu.` = c(0.64910, 0.64910, 0.64910, 0.64910),
  Max = c(1.00000, 1.00000, 1.00000, 1.00000),
  NA_s = c(1617, 1617, 1617, 1617),
  Grad_Rate = c(0.525, 0.502, 0.488, 0.505)
)

kable(data, "html") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = FALSE) %>%
  column_spec(2:9, width = "100px") %>%
  add_header_above(c(" ", "Descriptive Statistics" = 6, "Graduation Rate" = 2)) %>%
  add_footnote(c("Note: NA_s represents the number of missing values."))


#used notes from poli 30 to create rmarkdown that would create table 2
